summary.intent: Fix three CI failures in the moltis repository by identifying and resolving the root cause of failing E2E tests.
summary.outcome: All three CI failures were traced to a single root cause: a stale `isNoProvidersConfiguredResponse` helper in the E2E test that no longer matched the updated error shape after a `localizeRpcError` refactor. The fix was applied and committed.

repo: Commit 34395954 ('fix(web): preserve server error message for model probes') changed `localizeRpcError` in helpers.js to replace `error.message` with a locale string and move the original backend message to `error.serverMessage`.

The `isNoProvidersConfiguredResponse` helper in chat-input.spec.js is used to skip tests in CI environments where no LLM providers are configured — it gates tests on whether the backend returns a 'no LLM providers configured' error.
The settings-nav test failure in run 2 was flaky and passed on retry — unrelated to the main issue.
All three CI run failures resolved to the same underlying test failure: `chat-input.spec.js:225` — 'chat.full_context reports workspace prompt truncation'.

code.finding: Test 'chat.full_context reports workspace prompt truncation' failed because isNoProvidersConfiguredResponse only checked error.message, not error.serverMessage. Fixed to check both fields.
localizeRpcError replaces error.message with a localized string ('An internal server error occurred.') and stores the original backend message in error.serverMessage.

workflow: Playwright artifacts can be downloaded via `gh run download` to inspect trace files locally when CI logs are insufficient.
When a CI failure spans multiple runs with the same test name, it's worth checking git log for recent changes to shared helpers before diving into test logic.

friction: Downloading and parsing Playwright trace artifacts required multiple attempts and bash commands to extract useful signal.
The CI logs were truncated, requiring artifact download to find the actual test failure details.
The root cause was in a shared JS helper (`helpers.js`) changed weeks earlier — cross-referencing git history was necessary to connect the dots.
open_items: No regression test or comment was added to document why `isNoProvidersConfiguredResponse` checks both `message` and `serverMessage` — could confuse future maintainers.
The flaky settings-nav test (run 2) was not investigated since it passed on retry — underlying flakiness may still exist.
